order_sense+palework.name: Ashen Meridian
order_sense+palework.description: Where Order-Sense traced the invisible lattice of what-is and Palework read the pale threads of what-will-be, the Ashen Meridian fuses both into a single sovereign perception: the practitioner sees not merely the structure of the present moment nor the drift of futures, but the exact seam where fate is load-bearing — the one thread whose breaking would unravel a chain of events entirely. Ash…
order_sense+palework.notFor: The Ashen Meridian cannot heal what has already occurred, reverse damage already taken, or compel a person's will — it forecloses futures, it does not rewrite the past, and it holds no sway over the living heart.

tree.name: Pale Lattice Reading
tree.grants: The practitioner perceives both the structural order of a space or object and its probable futures simultaneously — a door is seen as a door and as the three most likely events that will pass…
tree.cannot: Cannot yet distinguish which future-thread is load-bearing; all probable threads appear with equal weight, making binding impossible at this rank.

tree.name: Meridian Strike
tree.grants: Strikes land not on the body but on the causal structure around a target — disrupting the ordered sequence of their intended action before it completes. A swordsman mid-swing finds the motion…
tree.cannot: Cannot hold the disruption beyond one action; the causal structure reasserts itself, and the practitioner cannot yet permanently foreclose any thread.

tree.name: Bound Meridian
tree.grants: The practitioner identifies the single load-bearing future-thread in any foreseeable chain and seals it — the foreseen event is bound from occurring for as long as concentration is maintained, or…
tree.cannot: Cannot bind more than one thread at a time; cannot bind events in which the practitioner is the sole cause; cannot bind death already in motion.

deathsense+order_sense.name: Undying Ledger
deathsense+order_sense.description: Where Deathsense reads the cold signatures of endings and Order-Sense traces the rigid lattice of what-must-be, The Undying Ledger fuses both into a single sovereign perception: the practitioner sees not merely that death approaches, nor merely that patterns hold their shape — but reads the precise architecture of fate itself, the numbered threads that bind each living thing to its appointed…
deathsense+order_sense.notFor: The Undying Ledger cannot create new fates, resurrect the dead, alter an entry already fulfilled, or perceive anything outside the axis of endings and structured order — it is blind to chaos, to love, to chance that has not yet…

tree.name: Pale Inscription
tree.grants: The practitioner reads the death-pattern of any creature within sight simultaneously — not sequentially as raw Deathsense demands — perceiving every nearby ending as a single illuminated page…
tree.cannot: Cannot yet distinguish which threads are mutable and which are fixed; all entries look equally inevitable at this rank.

tree.name: The Sealed Margin
tree.grants: The practitioner identifies the one moment in a target's near future where their fate-thread is thinnest and most legible. Intervening in that exact moment — a strike, a word, a touch — lands with…
tree.cannot: Cannot yet enforce the binding; the practitioner sees the appointed moment but cannot yet compel the target toward it against their will.

tree.name: Writ of Completion
tree.grants: The Ledger's true power made manifest: the practitioner binds a target to their own foreseen entry, tethering them to the Ledger's record of their end. The bound target finds every escape route…
tree.cannot: Cannot rewrite the entry itself — it can only enforce what is already recorded. A fate the Ledger does not yet contain cannot be bound. Binding two targets…
